categoryName: BOOM: Metabolic Overview I: General Reactions

questions.topicName: BOOM: Metabolic Overview I: General Reactions
questions.stem: A urine microscopy specimen shows these colorless envelope- or pyramid-shaped crystals. What are they?

explanation.summary: The classic envelope (Maltese-cross pyramid) morphology is characteristic of calcium oxalate dihydrate crystals, the most common urinary crystal and stone type, associated with hypercalciuria and ethylene glycol toxicity.
explanation.whyCorrect: The classic envelope (Maltese-cross pyramid) morphology is characteristic of calcium oxalate dihydrate crystals, the most common urinary crystal and stone type, associated with hypercalciuria and ethylene glycol toxicity.

questions.topicName: BOOM: Metabolic Overview I: General Reactions
questions.stem: Using this basic metabolic panel (Na+ 138, K+ 4.0, Cl- 102, HCO3- 10 mEq/L), what is the serum anion gap?

explanation.summary: Anion gap = Na+ - (Cl- + HCO3-) = 138 - (102 + 10) = 26 mEq/L. This markedly elevated gap with low bicarbonate indicates a high anion gap metabolic acidosis.
explanation.whyCorrect: Anion gap = Na+ - (Cl- + HCO3-) = 138 - (102 + 10) = 26 mEq/L. This markedly elevated gap with low bicarbonate indicates a high anion gap metabolic acidosis.

questions.topicName: BOOM: Metabolic Overview I: General Reactions
questions.stem: The diagram shows sequential oxidation of a fat-soluble vitamin. The final active metabolite drives embryonic development, tissue maintenance, and immunity by which primary molecular mechanism?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Functioning as a cofactor for gamma-carboxylation

choices.label: B
choices.text: Serving as the chromophore for photon absorption in rods

choices.label: D
choices.text: Acting as an enzymatic antioxidant in membranes

choices.label: E
choices.text: Binding nuclear receptors to regulate gene transcription

explanation.summary: Retinol is oxidized by ADH to retinaldehyde (vision, as the visual chromophore) and irreversibly by ALDH to retinoic acid. Retinoic acid binds nuclear RAR/RXR receptors to control gene transcription, driving embryonic development, epithelial maintenance, and immune function.
explanation.whyCorrect: Retinol is oxidized by ADH to retinaldehyde (vision, as the visual chromophore) and irreversibly by ALDH to retinoic acid. Retinoic acid binds nuclear RAR/RXR receptors to control gene transcription, driving embryonic development, epithelial maintenance, and immune function.

questions.topicName: BOOM: Metabolic Overview I: General Reactions
questions.stem: This enzyme interconverts pyruvate and lactate while cycling NADH and NAD+. Under anaerobic conditions, what is the main metabolic purpose of the forward (pyruvate to lactate) reaction?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Produce oxygen for the electron transport chain

choices.label: D
choices.text: Regenerate NAD+ so glycolysis can continue producing ATP

explanation.summary: Lactate dehydrogenase reduces pyruvate to lactate, oxidizing NADH back to NAD+. Regenerating NAD+ allows glycolysis (which needs NAD+ at the GAPDH step) to keep running and yield ATP when oxygen or mitochondria are unavailable.
explanation.whyCorrect: Lactate dehydrogenase reduces pyruvate to lactate, oxidizing NADH back to NAD+. Regenerating NAD+ allows glycolysis (which needs NAD+ at the GAPDH step) to keep running and yield ATP when oxygen or mitochondria are unavailable.

questions.topicName: BOOM: Metabolic Overview I: General Reactions
questions.stem: The alcohol dehydrogenase and acetaldehyde dehydrogenase steps in this pathway both consume NAD+ and generate NADH. What metabolic consequence does the resulting high NADH/NAD+ ratio promote?

explanation.summary: Ethanol oxidation raises the cytosolic NADH/NAD+ ratio, pushing pyruvate to lactate (lactic acidosis) and oxaloacetate to malate. Depletion of pyruvate and oxaloacetate impairs gluconeogenesis, causing hypoglycemia.
explanation.whyCorrect: Ethanol oxidation raises the cytosolic NADH/NAD+ ratio, pushing pyruvate to lactate (lactic acidosis) and oxaloacetate to malate. Depletion of pyruvate and oxaloacetate impairs gluconeogenesis, causing hypoglycemia.
